Next: Build 5.1-5.8 Syndromic Management.md

▼  Show infographic image click to expand
▸ 15.1.04 5 1 5 8 Syndromic Management

Parent Topic: STI MOC → STI Hierarchy

Status: full-fcps-mrcp-note

Priority: ⭐⭐⭐ HIGHEST (FCPS/MRCP — WHO Syndromic Management, Clinical Algorithms, Resource-Limited Settings)

Source: Davidson 24th Ed Ch 13; WHO Guidelines (2021); CDC/BASHH; FCPS/MRCP Syllabus

1. 🎯 Learning Objectives

  • [ ] Apply WHO Syndromic Management algorithms for all major STI syndromes
  • [ ] Manage Urethral Discharge Syndrome (Male & Female differentials)
  • [ ] Manage Vaginal Discharge Syndrome (BV, Candida, Trichomonas, GC/CT)
  • [ ] Manage Genital Ulcer Disease (GUD) (Syphilis, HSV, Chancroid, LGV, Donovanosis)
  • [ ] Manage Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (PID) (Diagnosis, Treatment, Complications)
  • [ ] Manage Epididymo-orchitis (GC/CT, Enteric, TB, Mumps)
  • [ ] Manage Anorectal STIs / Proctitis (GC/CT, LGV, HSV, Syphilis, Mpox, Shigella)
  • [ ] Manage Oropharyngeal STIs (GC/CT, HSV, Syphilis, HPV, Mpox)
  • [ ] Manage Neonatal STIs (Ophthalmia Neonatorum, Pneumonia, Congenital Syphilis)
  • [ ] Apply Partner Notification, Test of Cure, Follow-Up protocols
  • [ ] Answer viva: "Urethral Discharge Algorithm" and "PID Diagnosis" and "GUD Differential" and "Neonatal Ophthalmia"

2. 🧠 Core Concept: Syndromic Management Framework

⬇ Clinical Pathway
Syndromic Management
↓
Patient Presents with Syndrome
Syndromes
↓
Clinical Assessment
Empiric Treatment Covers Most Likely Pathogens
Urethral Discharge (M/F)
Vaginal Discharge
Genital Ulcer Disease (GUD)
Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (PID)
Epididymo-orchitis
Anorectal STIs/Proctitis
Oropharyngeal STIs
Neonatal STIs
↓
History (Sexual, Symptoms, Partners)
Examination (Genital, Inguinal, Systemic)
Point-of-Care Tests (NAAT, Microscopy, Serology)
Partner Notification & Treatment
Follow-Up & Test of Cure
Prevention Counselling (Condoms, Vaccination, PrEP)

5.1 Urethral Discharge Syndrome (Male)

Clinical Assessment

ComponentKey Elements
HistoryDysuria, Urethral Discharge (Purulent/Mucopurulent), Urinary Frequency, Sexual History (New/Multiple Partners, Condom Use, MSM), Previous STIs
ExaminationUrethral Discharge (Milk Urethra if Not Spontaneous), Meatal Erythema/Edema, Inguinal Lymphadenopathy, Testicular/Scrotal Exam (Epididymitis), Rectal/Pharyngeal Exam if MSM

Differential Diagnosis

PathogenClinical Clues
Neisseria gonorrhoeaeProfuse Purulent Discharge, Acute Onset (<5 Days), Gram-Neg Intracellular Diplococci on Smear
Chlamydia trachomatisMucopurulent/Mucoid Discharge, Subacute (1-3 Weeks), Often Asymptomatic
Mycoplasma genitaliumMucopurulent Discharge, Persistent/Recurrent NGU, Macrolide Resistance
Trichomonas vaginalisMild Dysuria, Scanty Discharge, Motile Flagellates on Microscopy
Ureaplasma/Mycoplasma spp.Controversial Pathogenicity, Often Asymptomatic
Non-InfectiousChemical Urethritis, Trauma, Foreign Body, Behçet's, Reactive Arthritis

WHO Syndromic Algorithm — Male Urethral Discharge

⬇ Clinical Pathway
Male with Urethral Discharge
↓
Microscopy (Urethral Smear) — Gram Stain
↓
Gram-Neg Intracellular Diplococci?
↓
& G
NAAT for GC/CT (If Available)
↓
Partner Notification + Treatment
NAAT Result
↓
Test of Cure: GC 2w, CT 4w, MG 4w
Counselling: Condoms, HIV/STI Screen, Vaccination
Consider MG/TV/UU Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7d (+/- Metronidazole 2g if TV Suspected)

Treatment Regimens (Empiric)

ScenarioRegimen
Gram-Neg Diplococci +ve (Presumptive GC)Ceftriaxone 500mg IM Stat + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7 Days
NAAT GC/CT +veCeftriaxone 500mg IM Stat + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7 Days
NGU (GC/CT Negative)Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7 Days (Covers CT, MG, UU)
Persistent/Recurrent NGUTest for MG (NAAT) → If +ve: Moxifloxacin 400mg OD × 7-14d; Add Metronidazole 2g Stat if TV Suspected
MSM3-Site Testing (Urethral, Pharyngeal, Rectal); Treat All Positive Sites

5.2 Vaginal Discharge Syndrome (Female)

Clinical Assessment

ComponentKey Elements
HistoryVaginal Discharge (Colour, Consistency, Odour), Vulval Pruritus/Burning, Dysuria, Dyspareunia, Intermenstrual/Post-Coital Bleeding, Contraception, Sexual History, Pregnancy Status
ExaminationVulval Inspection (Erythema, Oedema, Lesions), Speculum Exam (Cervix: Ectopy, Friability, Discharge, Strawberry Cervix), Bimanual Exam (Adnexal Tenderness, Cervical Motion Tenderness — PID Screen)
POC TestspH Paper, Whiff Test (KOH), Wet Mount (Saline/KOH), NAAT (GC/CT/TV/MG), Rapid Syphilis/HIV

Differential Diagnosis

SyndromeKey FeaturespHMicroscopyTreatment
Bacterial Vaginosis (BV)Thin Grey-White Homogeneous Discharge, Fishy Odour (Amine), No/Vague Inflammation>4.5Clue Cells, Few WBCsMetronidazole 400mg BD × 7d / Tinidazole 2g Stat
Vulvovaginal Candidiasis (VVC)Thick White "Cottage Cheese", Pruritus, Vulvar Erythema/Oedema, Satellite Lesions<4.5KOH: Hyphae/Pseudohyphae/BudsFluconazole 150mg Stat / Topical Azole
TrichomoniasisFrothy Yellow-Green, Strawberry Cervix, Vulval Erythema, Pruritus>4.5Wet Mount: Motile Trichomonads (Pear-Shaped)Metronidazole 2g Stat / Tinidazole 2g Stat
Gonorrhoea/ChlamydiaMucopurulent Cervicitis, Ectopy, Friability, ICB/IM BleedingNormal/↑NAAT GC/CTCeftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7d
Mixed/Co-infectionFeatures of MultipleVariableMultiple PositiveTreat All Identified

WHO Syndromic Algorithm — Female Vaginal Discharge

⬇ Clinical Pathway
Female with Vaginal Discharge
↓
Speculum Exam + pH + Whiff + Wet Mount (Saline/KOH)
↓
Microscopy Result
↓
& E & F & G & H
Treat for Candida Fluconazole 150mg Stat / Topical Azole
Treat for Trichomonas Metronidazole 2g Stat / Tinidazole 2g
NAAT GC/CT If +ve: Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 100mg BD × 7d
Treat Empirically for Most Likely (BV + Candida Common) OR Refer
↓
Partner Notification (If STI)
↓
Follow-Up / Test of Cure (GC 2w, CT 4w, TV 4w)
Counselling: Condoms, Contraception, HPV Vaccine, HIV Screen

Treatment Summary (Vaginal Discharge)

Syndrome1st LineAlternativePregnancy
BVMetronidazole 400mg BD × 7dTinidazole 2g Stat / Metronidazole 0.75% Gel × 5dMetronidazole 400mg BD × 7d (Safe)
Candida (Uncomplicated)Fluconazole 150mg StatClotrimazole 500mg Pessary × 1d / Miconazole 200mg × 3-7dTopical Azole Only (Clotrimazole 500mg × 7d)
TrichomonasMetronidazole 2g StatTinidazole 2g StatMetronidazole 2g Stat (Safe)
GC/CT CervicitisCeftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 100mg BD × 7dAzithromycin 1g Stat (If Doxy Contraindicated)Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Azithromycin 1g Stat

5.3 Genital Ulcer Disease (GUD) Syndrome

Clinical Assessment

ComponentKey Elements
HistoryUlcer Onset/Duration, Pain (Painless vs Painful), Number of Ulcers, Systemic Symptoms (Fever, Lymphadenopathy), Sexual History, HIV Status
ExaminationUlcer(s): Number, Size, Edge (Indurated/Undermined), Base (Clean/Purulent), Pain (Palpation), Induration, Lymphadenopathy (Regional: Inguinal/Femoral, Characteristics: Tender/Non-Tender, Suppurative/Non-Suppurative, Matted), Other Sites (Oral, Anal, Perianal)
POC TestsRPR/VDRL + TPPA (Syphilis), HSV PCR (Lesion Swab), H. ducreyi PCR (Chancroid), LGV PCR (C. trachomatis L1-3), Dark Field Microscopy (Syphilis)

Differential Diagnosis

PathogenUlcer CharacteristicsLymphadenopathyKey Features
Treponema pallidum (Syphilis)Single, Painless, Clean Base, Indurated, Sharply DemarcatedNon-Tender, Rubbery, Bilateral/RegionalChancre (Primary), Serology (RPR/VDRL + TPPA)
Herpes Simplex (HSV-1/2)Multiple, Painful, Vesicles → Shallow Ulcers, Irregular EdgesTender, BilateralVesicles → Ulcers, Recurrent, PCR +ve
Haemophilus ducreyi (Chancroid)Painful, Soft, Undermined Edges, Ragged, Purulent Base, Bleeds EasilySuppurative Buboes (Unilateral, Fluctuant, Tender, Mire)Multiple Ulcers, H. ducreyi PCR
LGV (C. trachomatis L1-3)Painless (Primary) → Proctitis/Buboes (Secondary)Suppurative Buboes (Groin/Femoral), FistulaeMSM, Proctocolitis, LGV PCR (L1-3)
Donovanosis (Klebsiella granulomatis)Progressive, Painless, Beefy Red, Granulomatous, Bleeds on TouchInguinal Lymphadenopathy (Rare)Endemic Areas, K. granulomatis PCR

WHO Syndromic Algorithm — GUD

⬇ Clinical Pathway
Patient with Genital Ulcer
↓
Clinical Assessment: Pain, Number, Edges, Base, Lymphadenopathy
↓
Ulcer Painless, Indurated, Clean Base + Non-Tender Lymphadenopathy?
↓
& F & H & J & K
Ulcer Painful, Vesicles → Ulcers, Tender Lymphadenopathy?
↓
RPR/VDRL + TPPA for ALL GUD
HSV Likely HSV PCR (Lesion Swab) Acyclovir 400mg TDS × 7-10d
Ulcer Painful, Soft, Undermined Edges, Purulent + Suppurative Buboes?
↓
Partner Notification + HIV Test
Chancroid Likely H. ducreyi PCR Azithromycin 1g Stat / Ceftriaxone 250mg IM
LGV Suspected (MSM, Proctitis, Buboes)?
↓
Follow-Up: Syphilis RPR 3/6/12m; HSV Suppressive if ≥6/yr
LGV PCR (L1-3) Doxycycline 100mg BD × 21d
Consider Donovanosis/Other Refer/Specialist

Treatment Summary (GUD)

SyndromeTreatmentNotes
Syphilis (Primary/Secondary/Early Latent)Benzathine Penicillin G 2.4MU IM Single DoseLate Latent/Tertiary: ×3 Weekly Doses
HSV (Primary/Recurrent)Acyclovir 400mg TDS × 7-10d (Primary) / ×5d (Recurrent)Suppressive if ≥6/yr: Valacyclovir 500mg OD
ChancroidAzithromycin 1g Stat / Ceftriaxone 250mg IMBubo Needle Aspiration
LGVDoxycycline 100mg BD × 21 DaysErythromycin if Pregnant
DonovanosisAzithromycin 1g Weekly × 3-4 Weeks / Doxycycline 100mg BD × 3 WeeksEndemic Areas

5.4 Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (PID) Syndrome

Diagnostic Criteria (CDC/WHO)

CriteriaMinimum Required
MajorLower Abdominal Tenderness, Adnexal Tenderness, Cervical Motion Tenderness (All 3 = High Specificity)
Minor/SupportiveFever >38.3°C, Abnormal Vaginal/Cervical Discharge, Leukocytosis, Elevated CRP/ESR, Microscopy: WBCs on Wet Mount, Positive NAAT GC/CT, Fever, Temperature >38°C
Diagnosis: Empiric Treatment if All Major Criteria Present + No Alternative Diagnosis; Low Threshold in Young Sexually Active Women

Clinical Assessment

ComponentKey Elements
HistoryLower Abdominal Pain (Bilateral), Abnormal Vaginal Discharge, Fever, Dyspareunia, Abnormal Bleeding, Recent Sexual Exposure, IUD Insertion, Previous PID/STI
ExaminationCervical Motion Tenderness, Adnexal Tenderness, Uterine Tenderness, Cervicitis (Mucopurulent Discharge, Ectopy, Friability), Adnexal Mass (TOA), Fever, Rebound Tenderness (Peritonitis)
InvestigationsNAAT GC/CT (Urine/Swab), RPR/VDRL, HSV/HIV, CBC, CRP, ESR, Urinalysis, Pregnancy Test (β-hCG), Transvaginal US (TOA, Hydrosalpinx, Free Fluid)

WHO Syndromic Algorithm — PID

⬇ Clinical Pathway
Woman with Lower Abdominal Pain
↓
Pelvic Exam: CMT + Adnexal Tenderness + CMT?
↓
& I & J
Additional Criteria: Fever, Discharge, Leukocytosis, CRP↑, GC/CT+
↓
Empiric Treatment: Ceftriaxone 500mg IM Stat + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 14d + Metronidazole 400mg BD × 14d (+/- Azithromycin 1g if MG Suspected)
Partner Notification + Treatment
≥1 Additional Criterion?
↓
Severe / TOA / Pregnancy / Failed Outpatient?
Test of Cure: GC 2w, CT 4w; Follow-Up 7d, 30d
Consider Alternative (Appendicitis, Ectopic, Endometriosis, UTI, IBS)
↓
Admit IV: Ceftriaxone 2g IV q24h + Doxycycline 100mg IV/PO BD + Metronidazole 500mg IV q8h (+/- Clindamycin if TOA)
Outpatient + Follow-Up 72h
Contraception Counselling, IUD Removal if Present, HIV/Syphilis Screen

Treatment Regimens

SettingRegimen
Outpatient (Mild-Moderate)Ceftriaxone 500mg IM Stat + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 14d + Metronidazole 400mg BD × 14d
Inpatient (Severe/TOA/Pregnancy/Failed OP)Ceftriaxone 2g IV q24h + Doxycycline 100mg IV/PO BD + Metronidazole 500mg IV q8h (+ Clindamycin 900mg IV q8h if TOA Suspected)
IUD In SituRemove IUD If Possible (After 1-2 Days Antibiotics)
TOA (Tubo-Ovarian Abscess)IV Antibiotics (As Above) + Image-Guided Drainage (If >5cm or Failed 48-72h Medical)

Complications & Sequelae

ComplicationIncidenceImpact
Infertility (Tubal Factor)10-15% per EpisodeCumulative (3 Episodes → 50-75%)
Ectopic Pregnancy6-10x Increased RiskLife-Threatening
Chronic Pelvic Pain18-30%Quality of Life Impact
TOA5-10%Sepsis Risk, May Need Drainage/Surgery
Fitz-Hugh-Curtis Syndrome4-14%Perihepatitis, RUQ Pain, Violin String Adhesions

5.5 Epididymo-orchitis Syndrome

Clinical Assessment

ComponentKey Elements
HistoryAcute Scrotal Pain (Unilateral), Swelling, Dysuria, Urethral Discharge, Fever, Sexual History, Trauma, Mumps Exposure, TB Risk
ExaminationTender, Swollen Epididymis/Testis, Erythema/Oedema Scrotum, Prehn's Sign (Relief on Elevation), Cremasteric Reflex Absent, Urethral Discharge, Inguinal Lymphadenopathy
InvestigationsNAAT GC/CT (Urine/Urethral Swab), Urine Culture, Scrotal Doppler US (Rule Out Torsion), RPR/VDRL, HIV, TB Screen (If Chronic/Atypical)

Differential Diagnosis

AetiologyAge GroupKey Features
GC/CT (STI)<35 YearsUrethral Discharge, Acute Onset, Sexually Active
Enteric Organisms (E. coli, Pseudomonas)>35 Years / Urinary Abnormalities / CatheterNo Urethral Discharge, Urinary Symptoms, Recurrent UTI
MumpsPost-Pubertal MalesParotitis Precedes Orchitis (4-8 Days), Bilateral (20-30%)
TBEndemic Areas / ImmunocompromisedChronic, Painless, Constitutional Symptoms, Positive Mantoux/IGRA
Behçet'sYoung AdultsOral/Genital Ulcers, Uveitis, Pathergy
Torsion (Surgical Emergency)AdolescentsSudden Severe Pain, High-Riding Testis, Absent Cremasteric Reflex, Urgent US

Treatment

AetiologyRegimen
GC/CT (STI)Ceftriaxone 500mg IM Stat + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 10-14 Days
Enteric (E. coli, Pseudomonas)Ciprofloxacin 500mg BD × 10-14d / Ofloxacin 200mg BD × 10-14d
MumpsSupportive (Analgesia, Scrotal Support, Ice Packs)
TBStandard Anti-TB Regimen (6 Months)
Supportive (All)Scrotal Elevation, Ice Packs (Intermittent), NSAIDs/Opioids, Scrotal Support

5.6 Anorectal STIs / Proctitis Syndrome

Clinical Assessment

ComponentKey Elements
HistoryAnal/Rectal Pain, Discharge, Bleeding, Tenesmus, Diarrhoea/Constipation, Sexual History (MSM, Receptive Anal), HIV Status
ExaminationPerianal Inspection (Lesions, Warts, Ulcers, Fissures, Tags), Digital Rectal Exam (Tenderness, Mass, Blood), Anoscopy/Proctoscopy (Mucosal Erythema, Ulcers, Discharge, Ulcers, Masses)
InvestigationsNAAT GC/CT/LGV/HSV (Rectal Swab), RPR/VDRL, HIV, Shigella Culture/PCR, Mpox PCR

Differential Diagnosis (MSM Focus)

PathogenClinical Features
GC/CTMucopurulent Discharge, Mild Proctitis
LGV (C. trachomatis L1-3)Proctocolitis (Rectal Pain, Bloody Mucus, Tenesmus, Constipation), Suppurative Inguinal Buboes
HSVVesicles → Ulcers, Painful, Recurrent
SyphilisPainless Ulcer (Primary), Mucous Patches (Secondary)
MpoxGenital/Perianal Lesions, Proctitis, Lymphadenopathy
ShigellaDysentery (Bloody Diarrhoea, Fever, Tenesmus)

WHO Syndromic Algorithm — Proctitis (MSM)

⬇ Clinical Pathway
MSM with Proctitis
↓
Anoscopy + Rectal Swabs
↓
NAAT: GC/CT/LGV/HSV + RPR/VDRL + HIV
↓
LGV PCR +ve?
↓
& G & I & K & L
GC/CT +ve?
↓
Partner Notification + HIV/Syphilis Screen
Treat GC/CT: Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 100mg BD × 7d
HSV PCR +ve?
↓
Follow-Up: LGV 4-6w, GC/CT 2w/4w, HSV Suppressive if ≥6/yr
Treat HSV: Acyclovir 400mg TDS × 7-10d
Shigella PCR/Culture +ve?
↓
Treat Shigella: Ciprofloxacin 500mg BD × 5d / Azithromycin 500mg BD × 3d
Consider Mpox, Syphilis, IBD, Other

Treatment Summary (Proctitis)

PathogenTreatment
LGVDoxycycline 100mg BD × 21 Days
GC/CTCeftriaxone 500mg IM Stat + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7 Days
HSVAcyclovir 400mg TDS × 7-10 Days (Suppressive if Recurrent)
SyphilisStage-Appropriate Penicillin
MpoxSupportive / Tecovirimat if Severe
ShigellaCiprofloxacin 500mg BD × 5d / Azithromycin 500mg BD × 3d

5.7 Oropharyngeal STIs Syndrome

Clinical Assessment

ComponentKey Elements
HistorySore Throat, Dysphagia, Odynophagia, Cervical Lymphadenopathy, Oral Lesions, Sexual History (Oral Sex, MSM), HIV Status
ExaminationOropharyngeal Exam (Tonsillar Exudate, Ulcers, Vesicles, Erythema, Lymphadenopathy), Genital Exam (Concurrent STI Screen)
InvestigationsNAAT GC/CT (Pharyngeal Swab), HSV PCR (Lesion Swab), RPR/VDRL, HIV, Mpox PCR (If Lesions)

Differential & Treatment

PathogenFeaturesTreatment
GC (Pharyngeal Gonorrhoea)Often Asymptomatic; Sore Throat, ExudateCeftriaxone 1g IM Stat + Azithromycin 1g Stat; TOC Mandatory at 2 Weeks
CT (Pharyngeal Chlamydia)Rare SymptomaticDoxycycline 100mg BD × 7 Days / Azithromycin 1g Stat
HSVVesicles → Ulcers, PainfulAcyclovir 400mg TDS × 7-10d
SyphilisMucous Patches, Snail-Track UlcersStage-Appropriate Penicillin
MpoxOral/Perioral LesionsSupportive / Tecovirimat if Severe
HPVOral Warts (Rare), Oropharyngeal CancerSurveillance / Vaccination
Key: Pharyngeal GC → Higher Ceftriaxone Dose (1g IM) + Mandatory TOC at 2 Weeks

5.8 Neonatal STIs Syndrome

Clinical Assessment

ComponentKey Elements
HistoryMaternal STI Status (Syphilis, GV, CT, HSV, HBV, HCV, HIV), Delivery Mode, Gestational Age, Birth Weight, Breastfeeding
ExaminationConjunctiva (Discharge, Redness, Swelling), Skin (Rash, Vesicles, Ulcers, Pustules), Oral Mucosa (White Patches, Ulcers), Genitalia (Discharge, Lesions), Neurological (Tone, Reflexes, Seizures), Respiratory (Distress, Pneumonia), Growth Parameters

Neonatal STI Syndromes

SyndromeKey FeaturesAetiologyUrgent Action
Ophthalmia NeonatorumConjunctival Injection, Swelling, Discharge (Purulent/Mucopurulent)GC (Day 2-5, Hyperacute), CT (Day 5-14, Mucopurulent), HSV (Day 7-14, Vesicles), Chemical (Day 1, Mild)Urgent Eye Swabs (GC/CT/HSV PCR, Gram Stain, Culture) → Empiric Ceftriaxone 50mg/kg IM/IV Single Dose (Pending Results)
Neonatal HSVSEM (Skin/Eye/Mouth), CNS (Encephalitis), DIS (Disseminated)HSV-1/2 (Vertical Transmission)IV Acyclovir 20mg/kg IV q8h (14d SEM, 21d CNS/DIS + 6mo Oral Suppression)
Neonatal Pneumonia (CT)Staccato Cough, Tachypnoea, Hypoxaemia, 3-16 WeeksChlamydia trachomatisAzithromycin 20mg/kg OD × 10-14 Days
Congenital SyphilisSnuffles, Rash, Hepatosplenomegaly, Osteochondritis, PseudoparalysisTreponema pallidumPenicillin G IV 50,000 U/kg q12h × 7d → q8h × 3d (10 Days Total)
Neonatal Gonorrhoea (Scalp Abscess)Fluctuant Scalp Swelling (Fetal Scalp Electrode/Forceps)Neisseria gonorrhoeaeI&D + Ceftriaxone 50mg/kg IM/IV × 7 Days

Neonatal Ophthalmia — Empiric Algorithm

⬇ Clinical Pathway
Neonate with Conjunctivitis
↓
Urgent Swabs: Gram Stain, GC/CT/HSV PCR, Culture
↓
Early/Severe (Day 2-5, Hyperacute)?
↓
& F & G
Mucopurulent (Day 5-14)?
↓
Definitive Treatment Based on PCR/Culture Results
Empiric Ceftriaxone 50mg/kg IM/IV Single Dose + Azithromycin 20mg/kg OD × 3d (If CT Suspected)
Consider HSV (Vesicles), Chemical (Day 1), Other
↓
Parental STI Screen + Partner Notification

Partner Notification — Neonatal STIs

ScenarioAction
Neonatal GC/CT/HSV/SyphilisMaternal + Partner(s) Screening & Treatment
Maternal STI IdentifiedFull STI Screen (HIV, Syphilis, HBV, HCV, GC, CT, HSV, Trichomonas)
Partner(s)Treat as Per Maternal Infection

3. ⚡ FCPS/MRCP High-Yield Summary

SyndromeKey Algorithm Points
Urethral Discharge (M)Gram-Neg Diplococci → GC+CT (Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 7d); NAAT GC/CT+ve → Same; GC/CT-ve → Doxy 7d (NGU)
Vaginal DischargeBV: Clue Cells/Whiff+ → Metro/Tini; Candida: KOH Hyphae → Fluconazole/Topical; Trich: Motile Trichomonas → Metro/Tini 2g; Cervicitis: GC/CT NAAT → Ceftriaxone + Doxy
GUDPainless+Indurated+Non-Tender LAD = Syphilis (BPC 2.4MU IM); Painful+Vesicles = HSV (Acyclovir); Painful+Undermined+Suppurative Buboes = Chancroid (Azi 1g/Ceftriaxone); LGV: Doxy 21d
PIDCMT + Adnexal Tenderness + Uterine Tenderness = Empiric Tx → Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 14d + Metro 14d
Epididymo-orchitis<35y: GC/CT (Ceftriaxone + Doxy 10-14d); >35y/Urinary Abnormalities: Enteric (Cipro/Ofloxacin 10-14d)
Proctitis (MSM)LGV (Doxy 21d) > GC/CT (Ceftriaxone+Doxy) > HSV (Acyclovir) > Shigella (Cipro/Azi)
Neonatal OphthalmiaDay 2-5 Hyperacute = GC (Ceftriaxone 50mg/kg IM); Day 5-14 = CT (Azi 20mg/kg OD × 3d); HSV = IV Aci 20mg/kg q8h
Partner NotificationAll STI Syndromes: Treat Partners (Last 60d GC/CT/Trich, 3-12m Syphilis, 60d HSV/HPV)
Test of CureGC: 2 Weeks; CT/TV/MG: 4 Weeks; LGV: 4-6 Weeks; Syphilis: RPR 3/6/12m

4. 🎤 Viva Questions (Expected Answers)

#QuestionExpected Answer
1Male urethral discharge — WHO syndromic algorithm?Gram Stain → Gram-Neg Diplococci → Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 7d; GC/CT NAAT+ve → Same; GC/CT-ve → Doxy 7d (NGU)
2Female vaginal discharge — syndromic approach?Speculum + pH + Whiff + Wet Mount; Clue Cells/Whiff+ = BV (Metro/Tini); KOH Hyphae = Candida (Fluconazole/Topical); Motile Trichomonads = Trich (Metro/Tini 2g); Cervicitis = GC/CT NAAT → Ceftriaxone + Doxy
3Genital ulcer — differential diagnosis and management?Painless+Indurated+Non-Tender LAD = Syphilis (BPC Single Dose); Painful+Vesicles = HSV (Acyclovir); Painful+Undermined+Suppurative Buboes = Chancroid (Azi/Ceftriaxone); LGV = Proctitis/Buboes (Doxy 21d)
4PID — diagnostic criteria and empirical treatment?CMT + Adnexal Tenderness + Uterine Tenderness = Empiric Tx; Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 14d + Metro 14d; Severe/TOA/Preg = IV Ceftriaxone 2g + Doxy IV + Metro IV
5. Epididymo-orchitis — aetiology by age and treatment?<35y: GC/CT → Ceftriaxone + Doxy 10-14d; >35y/Urinary Abnormalities: Enteric (Cipro/Ofloxacin 10-14d); Mumps: Supportive; TB: Anti-TB
6. Proctitis in MSM — syndromic algorithm?Anoscopy + Rectal Swabs (GC/CT/LGV/HSV/Shigella PCR + RPR + HIV); LGV PCR+ → Doxy 21d; GC/CT+ → Ceftriaxone+Doxy; HSV+ → Acyclovir; Shigella → Cipro/Azi
7. Neonatal ophthalmia — aetiology by age and treatment?Day 2-5 Hyperacute = GC (Ceftriaxone 50mg/kg IM); Day 5-14 = CT (Azi 20mg/kg OD × 3d); HSV = IV Aci 20mg/kg q8h × 14-21d
8. Partner notification — lookback periods?GC/CT/Trich: 60 Days; Syphilis: 1° 3m, 2° 6m, Early Latent 1y, Late Latent Long-Term Partners; HSV/HPV: 60 Days
9. Test of Cure intervals?GC: 2 Weeks; CT/TV/MG: 4 Weeks; LGV: 4-6 Weeks; Syphilis: RPR 3/6/12 Months
10. Neonatal HSV — classification and IV acyclovir duration?SEM: 14 Days; CNS: 21 Days + 6 Months Oral Suppression; DIS: 21 Days; IV Acyclovir 20mg/kg q8h

5. 🧩 Confusions & Mnemonics

ConfusionClarification
"All Urethral Discharge = GC"NO. NGU (CT, MG, TV) Common; Gram Stain/NAAT Differentiates
"All Vaginal Discharge = Candida"NO. BV Most Common; Trichomonas Common; Co-infections Frequent
"PID = Only STI"NO. Endogenous Flora (Anaerobes, GBS) Also Cause; Empiric Covers Polymicrobial
"All GUD = Syphilis"NO. HSV Most Common in Many Settings; Chancroid, LGV, Donovanosis Also
"Epididymitis = Always STI in Young Men"NO. Enteric Organisms in Older Men/Urinary Abnormalities
"Proctitis = Only LGV in MSM"NO. GC/CT Most Common; HSV, Syphilis, Shigella, Mpox Also
"Neonatal Ophthalmia = Only GC"NO. CT (Day 5-14), HSV (Day 7-14), Chemical (Day 1)
"PID = Always Needs Admission"NO. Mild-Moderate = Outpatient (Ceftriaxone IM + Doxy + Metro)
"All Scrotal Pain = Torsion"NO. Epididymitis Common; Torsion = Surgical Emergency (Absent Cremasteric, High-Riding)
"All Neonatal Eye Discharge = GC"NO. CT (Day 5-14), HSV (Day 7-14), Chemical (Day 1)
Mnemonic: SYNDROMIC MASTER ALGORITHMS
Syndromic: Treat Syndrome Empirically (Cover Most Likely Pathogens), Confirm Later
Y (Why Syndromic?): Resource-Limited, Rapid, Covers Co-Infections, Public Health Impact
Next Step: Partner Notification (Lookback: GC/CT/Trich 60d, Syphilis 3m-1y+, HSV/HPV 60d)
Discharge Male: Gram Stain → GC+CT (Ceftriaxone+Doxy); NAAT; NGU=Doxy
Reproductive Female: BV (Clue/Whiff=Metro), Candida (KOH=Fluc), Trich (Motile=Metro), Cervicitis (GC/CT=Ceph+Doxy)
Organism GUD: Syphilis (Painless+Indurated=BPC), HSV (Painful+Vesicles=Aci), Chancroid (Painful+Bubo=Azi), LGV (Proctitis=Doxy21d)
MID: CMT+Adnexal+CMT = PID → Ceftriaxone+Doxy+Metro
In Epididymitis: <35y=GC/CT (Ceph+Doxy); >35y=Enteric (Cipro)
Condition Proctitis MSM: LGV (Doxy21d) > GC/CT (Ceph+Doxy) > HSV (Aci) > Shigella (Cipro)
Neonatal Eye: Day 2-5 GC (Ceph 50mg/kg); Day 5-14 CT (Azi); HSV (IV Aci)
Infant HSV: SEM 14d, CNS 21d+6mo Oral, DIS 21d (IV Aci 20mg/kg q8h)
Condition PID: CMT+Adnexal+CMT = Empiric Tx
All STIs: Partner Notify (GC/CT/Trich 60d; Syphilis 3m-1y+; HSV/HPV 60d)
Test of Cure: GC 2w, CT/TV/MG 4w, LGV 4-6w, Syphilis RPR 3/6/12m
Stage Syphilis: 1°=Chancre (BPC Single); 2°=Rash (BPC Single); Early Latent (BPC Single); Late Latent/Tertiary (BPC ×3w); Neuro (Pen G IV 10-14d); Congenital (Pen G IV 10d)

6. 🗺️ Mind Map

Syndromic Management
Urethral Discharge (M)
Gram Stain → GC+CT → Ceph+Doxy
NAAT → GC/CT → Ceph+Doxy
NGU → Doxy
Vaginal Discharge (F)
BV: Clue/Whiff → Metro
Candida: KOH Hyphae → Fluconazole
Trich: Motile → Metro/Tini
Cervicitis: GC/CT NAAT → Ceph+Doxy
Genital Ulcer Disease
Syphilis: Painless/Indurated → BPC
HSV: Painful/Vesicles → Aci
Chancroid: Painful/Bubo → Azi
LGV: Proctitis → Doxy 21d
PID
CMT+Adnexal+CMT → Ceph+Doxy+Metro
Severe: IV Ceph 2g + Doxy IV + Metro IV
Epididymitis
<35y: GC/CT → Ceph+Doxy
>35y: Enteric → Cipro
Proctitis (MSM)
LGV: Doxy 21d
GC/CT: Ceph+Doxy
HSV: Aci
Shigella: Cipro
Neonatal
Eye: Day 2-5 GC (Ceph 50mg/kg); Day 5-14 CT (Azi); HSV (IV Aci)
HSV: SEM 14d, CNS 21d+6mo Oral
Congenital Syphilis: Pen G IV 10d
Partner Notification
GC/CT/Trich: 60d
Syphilis: 3m-1y+
HSV/HPV: 60d
Test of Cure
GC: 2w; CT/TV/MG: 4w; LGV: 4-6w; Syphilis: 3/6/12m

7. 📅 Spaced Repetition Tracker

ReviewDateScore (0–5)Notes
Day 1
Day 3
Day 7
Day 14
Day 30
Day 90

8. 📝 Self-Test Scorecard

SectionMaxScore%
Urethral Discharge (M)3
Vaginal Discharge (F)3
Genital Ulcer Disease3
PID3
Epididymo-orchitis2
Proctitis (MSM)2
Neonatal STIs3
Partner Notification & TOC2
Total20

9. 💬 Exam Answer Modes

FormatPromptKey Points
Long Essay"Describe the WHO syndromic management of male urethral discharge, female vaginal discharge, and genital ulcer disease."Urethral (M): Gram Stain → GC+CT (Ceph+Doxy); NAAT; NGU=Doxy; Vaginal (F): BV (Clue/Whiff=Metro), Candida (KOH=Fluconazole), Trich (Motile=Metro), Cervicitis=GC/CT NAAT; GUD: Syphilis (Painless/Indurated=BPC), HSV (Painful/Vesicles=Aci), Chancroid (Painful+Bubo=Azi), LGV (Doxy21d)
Short Note"WHO syndromic management of PID."Diagnosis: CMT + Adnexal Tenderness + Uterine Tenderness = Empiric Tx; Outpatient: Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 14d + Metro 14d; Inpatient: IV Ceftriaxone 2g + Doxy IV + Metro IV (+ Clindamycin if TOA)
Viva"Young man with urethral discharge. Gram stain shows intracellular Gram-negative diplococci. Management?"Presumptive GC → Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7d; NAAT GC/CT + Culture; Partner Notification (60d); TOC at 2 Weeks (NAAT)
Ward Round"25F with lower abdominal pain, cervical motion tenderness, adnexal tenderness, mucopurulent discharge. Management?"PID (Mild-Moderate) → Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 100mg BD × 14d + Metronidazole 400mg BD × 14d; Partner Notification (60d); TOC GC 2w, CT 4w; Remove IUD if Present
Last-Night"Urethral M: Gram Stain GC+CT → Ceph+Doxy; NGU=Doxy. Vaginal F: BV=Metro/Tini, Candida=Fluconazole, Trich=Metro/Tini, Cervicitis=Ceph+Doxy. GUD: Syph=Painless/BPC, HSV=Painful/Aci, Chancroid=Painful+Bubo/Azi, LGV=Doxy21d. PID: CMT+Adnexal+CMT → Ceph+Doxy+Metro. Epididymitis <35y=Ceph+Doxy, >35y=Cipro. Proctitis MSM: LGV>Doxy21d, GC/CT=Ceph+Doxy, HSV=Aci. Neonatal Eye: Day2-5=GC(Ceph50mg/kg), Day5-14=CT(Azi), HSV=IV Aci. Partner Notify: GC/CT/Trich 60d, Syphilis 3m-1y+, HSV 60d. TOC: GC 2w, CT/TV/MG 4w, Syphilis RPR 3/6/12m."Compressed.

10. 📌 Summary

  • Urethral Discharge (M): Gram Stain → GC+CT (Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 7d); NAAT GC/CT+ve → Same; GC/CT-ve → Doxy 7d (NGU)
  • Vaginal Discharge (F): BV: Clue Cells/Whiff+ → Metro/Tini; Candida: KOH Hyphae → Fluconazole 150mg/Topical; Trichomonas: Motile Flagellates → Metro/Tini 2g; Cervicitis: GC/CT NAAT+ve → Ceftriaxone + Doxy
  • GUD: Syphilis (Painless, Indurated, Non-Tender LAD) → BPC 2.4MU IM; HSV (Painful Vesicles) → Acyclovir; Chancroid (Painful, Undermined, Suppurative Buboes) → Azithromycin 1g/Ceftriaxone; LGV (Proctitis/Buboes) → Doxycycline 21d
  • PID: CMT + Adnexal + Uterine Tenderness = Empiric Tx → Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 14d + Metro 14d
  • Epididymo-orchitis: <35y: GC/CT → Ceftriaxone + Doxy 10-14d; >35y/Urinary Abnormalities: Enteric → Ciprofloxacin/Ofloxacin 10-14d
  • Proctitis (MSM): LGV (Doxy 21d) > GC/CT (Ceftriaxone+Doxy) > HSV (Acyclovir) > Shigella (Cipro/Azi)
  • Neonatal Ophthalmia: Day 2-5 = GC (Ceftriaxone 50mg/kg IM); Day 5-14 = CT (Azithromycin 20mg/kg OD × 3d); HSV = IV Acyclovir 20mg/kg q8h
  • Partner Notification: GC/CT/Trich: 60 Days; Syphilis: 1° 3m, 2° 6m, Early Latent 1y, Late Latent Long-Term; HSV/HPV: 60 Days
  • Test of Cure: GC: 2 Weeks; CT/TV/MG: 4 Weeks; LGV: 4-6 Weeks; Syphilis: RPR 3/6/12 Months

11. ❓ MCQs (10)

  1. Male urethral discharge — Gram stain shows intracellular Gram-negative diplococci. Empiric treatment?

A. Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7d B. Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7d C. Azithromycin 1g Stat D. Ciprofloxacin 500mg BD

Answer: B. Dual therapy for presumptive GC + CT.

  1. Vaginal discharge — clue cells present, positive whiff test. Diagnosis and treatment?

A. Candidiasis, Fluconazole C. Bacterial Vaginosis, Metronidazole 400mg BD × 7d C. Trichomoniasis, Metronidazole 2g D. Gonorrhoea, Ceftriaxone

Answer: B. BV = Clue Cells + Whiff Test + pH>4.5 → Metronidazole/Tinidazole.

  1. Genital ulcer — painful, undermined edges, purulent base, suppurative inguinal buboes. Diagnosis?

A. Primary Syphilis B. HSV C. Chancroid D. LGV

Answer: C. Chancroid = Painful, Soft/Undermined Ulcers, Suppurative Buboes.

  1. Pelvic Inflammatory Disease — minimum diagnostic criteria?

A. Lower Abdominal Pain Only B. Cervical Motion Tenderness + Adnexal Tenderness + Uterine Tenderness C. Fever + Discharge D. Positive NAAT for GC/CT

Answer: B. All Three Major Criteria (CMT + Adnexal + Uterine Tenderness) = Empiric PID Treatment.

  1. Pharyngeal gonorrhoea in MSM — treatment and follow-up?

A. Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + TOC 4w B. Ceftriaxone 1g IM + Azithromycin 1g + TOC at 2 Weeks (Mandatory) C. Doxycycline 7d D. Azithromycin 1g Only

Answer: B. Pharyngeal GC = Higher Ceftriaxone Dose (1g IM) + Mandatory TOC at 2 Weeks.

  1. Epididymo-orchitis in 40-year-old man with urinary symptoms. Likely aetiology & treatment?

A. GC/CT, Ceftriaxone + Doxy B. Enteric Organisms, Ciprofloxacin 500mg BD × 10-14d C. Mumps, Supportive D. TB, Anti-TB

Answer: B. >35y with Urinary Symptoms → Enteric Organisms (E. coli) → Ciprofloxacin/Ofloxacin 10-14d.

  1. MSM with proctitis, rectal pain, bloody mucus, tenesmus. LGV PCR positive. Treatment?

A. Ceftriaxone + Doxy 7d B. Doxycycline 100mg BD × 21 Days C. Acyclovir 400mg TDS × 7d D. Azithromycin 1g

Answer: B. LGV Confirmed → Doxycycline 100mg BD × 21 Days.

  1. Neonatal ophthalmia at Day 3, hyperacute, copious purulent discharge. Most likely aetiology?

A. Chlamydia B. Gonorrhoea C. HSV D. Chemical

Answer: B. Gonorrhoea = Day 2-5, Hyperacute, Copious Purulent.

  1. Test of Cure for genital Chlamydia — recommended interval?

A. 1 Week B. 2 Weeks C. 4 Weeks D. 8 Weeks

Answer: C. 4 Weeks Post-Treatment (NAAT); Avoid <3 Weeks (Residual DNA).

  1. Partner notification for early latent syphilis — lookback period?

A. 3 Months B. 6 Months C. 1 Year D. 2 Years

Answer: C. Early Latent Syphilis (<1 Year) → Lookback 1 Year.

  1. Neonatal HSV — CNS disease treatment duration?

A. 14 Days B. 21 Days IV Acyclovir + 6 Months Oral Suppression C. 10 Days D. 7 Days

Answer: B. CNS HSV: IV Acyclovir 20mg/kg q8h × 21 Days + Oral Suppression × 6 Months.

12. 📋 SBAs (10)

  1. Man with urethral discharge. Gram stain: intracellular Gram-negative diplococci. Empiric treatment?

A. Doxycycline Only B. Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 7d C. Azithromycin 1g D. Ciprofloxacin 500mg BD

Answer: B. Dual Therapy for Presumptive GC + CT.

  1. Woman with vaginal discharge, clue cells+, positive whiff test, pH 5.0. Treatment?

A. Fluconazole 150mg B. Metronidazole 400mg BD × 7d C. Ceftriaxone IM D. Azithromycin 1g

Answer: B. BV → Metronidazole 400mg BD × 7d (or Tinidazole 2g Stat).

  1. MSM with proctitis, LGV PCR positive. Treatment?

A. Ceftriaxone + Doxy 7d B. Doxycycline 100mg BD × 21 Days C. Azithromycin 1g D. Acyclovir

Answer: B. LGV → Doxycycline 100mg BD × 21 Days.

  1. PID — outpatient empirical regimen?

A. Ceftriaxone 500mg IM Alone B. Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxycycline 100mg BD × 14d + Metronidazole 400mg BD × 14d C. Doxycycline + Metronidazole D. Azithromycin + Metronidazole

Answer: B. Ceftriaxone 500mg IM + Doxy 14d + Metro 14d (Standard Outpatient Regimen).

  1. Neonate with mucopurulent conjunctivitis at Day 10. Most likely aetiology?

A. Gonorrhoea B. Chlamydia trachomatis C. HSV D. Chemical

Answer: B. Chlamydia = Day 5-14 Onset, Mucopurulent Discharge.

13. 🔑 Answer Keys

MCQsSBAs
1-B, 2-C, 3-C, 4-B, 5-B, 6-B, 7-B, 8-B, 9-B, 10-B1-B, 2-B, 3-B, 4-B, 5-B

▸ 15.1.05 6 Hiv Aids Cross Reference

Parent Topic: STI MOC → STI Hierarchy

Status: full-fcps-mrcp-note

Priority: ⭐⭐⭐ HIGHEST (FCPS/MRCP — HIV-STI Synergy, Co-infection Management, Immune Reconstitution, DAA/ART Interactions)

Source: Davidson 24th Ed Ch 12 & 13; WHO/BHIVA/EACS Guidelines; FCPS/MRCP Syllabus

1. 🎯 Learning Objectives

  • [ ] Understand HIV-STI Synergy (Biological Mechanisms, Epidemiological Overlap)
  • [ ] Apply STI Screening & Management in HIV (Enhanced Frequency, Atypical Presentations, Immune Reconstitution)
  • [ ] Manage STI Co-infections in HIV (Syphilis, HSV, HPV, HCV, Mpox, Cryptosporidium, Microsporidia)
  • [ ] Apply ART & DAA / STI Drug Interactions (CYP3A4, Transporters, Overlapping Toxicities)
  • [ ] Manage Immune Reconstitution Inflammatory Syndrome (IRIS) related to STIs
  • [ ] Apply PrEP/PEP Impact on STI Epidemiology
  • [ ] Answer viva: "HIV-Syphilis Co-infection" and "HSV in HIV" and "IRIS Management" and "PrEP & STI Rates"

2. 🧠 Core Concept: HIV-STI Synergy Framework

⬇ Clinical Pathway
HIV-STI Synergy
↓
Biological Mechanisms
Clinical Implications
Public Health Response
↓
Genital Mucosal Disruption → ↑ HIV Acquisition/Transmission
HIV Immunosuppression → ↑ STI Acquisition/Reactivation/Severity
Shared Risk Behaviours (MSM, Multiple Partners, Chemsex)
Concentric Epidemics (Network Interconnectedness)
Atypical STI Presentations in HIV
Accelerated STI Progression in HIV
IRIS (Immune Reconstitution Inflammatory Syndrome)
Drug Interactions (ART ↔ STI Tx)
Integrated STI/HIV Services
Combined Screening/Testing
PrEP/PEP + STI Risk Reduction
Partner Notification Integrated

1️⃣ HIV-STI Co-infection Epidemiology

STIHIV Co-infection ImpactKey Statistics
Syphilis↑ Acquisition (2-5x), ↑ Transmission, Atypical Serology, Rapid Progression, Neurosyphilis More CommonMSM: 20-40% Syphilis in HIV+; Syphilis ↑ HIV Viral Load
HSV-2↑ Acquisition (3x), ↑ Shedding, Severe/Recurrent, Ulcers Facilitate HIV60-80% HIV+ MSM HSV-2+; HSV-2 ↑ HIV Acquisition 3x
HPV↑ Persistence, ↑ CIN2/3 Progression, ↑ Anal/Oropharyngeal CancerHIV+ Women: 2-3x CIN2+; Anal Cancer 80-100x HIV+ MSM
HCV↑ Sexual Transmission (MSM, HIV+); Accelerated Fibrosis; DAA Cure = SVRMSM HIV+: HCV Incidence 5-10x Higher
MpoxCD4<200 = Severe, Prolonged, Disseminated2022 Outbreak: 40-50% Cases HIV+
Cryptosporidium/MicrosporidiaChronic Diarrhoea (HIV CD4<100)ART = Cure (Immune Reconstitution)
Mycobacterium tuberculosisTB-IRIS, Disseminated TBLeading Cause of Death in HIV

2️⃣ STI Screening in HIV — Enhanced Protocols

Screening Frequency (BHIVA/EACS/WHO)

PopulationScreening IntervalTests
All HIV+At Diagnosis + AnnuallySyphilis (RPR/VDRL+TPPA), HCV (Anti-HCV+RNA), HBV (HBsAg/Anti-HBs), HSV (Clinical), Chlamydia/Gonorrhoea (NAAT 3-Site if MSM)
MSMEvery 3-6 Months3-Site NAAT (Urethral, Pharyngeal, Rectal) GC/CT; Syphilis (RPR/VDRL+TPPA) Every 3-6m; HCV RNA Annually
Sex WorkersEvery 3 MonthsFull STI Screen (GC/CT 3-Site, Syphilis, HIV, HBV, HCV, HSV, Trichomonas)
Pregnant HIV+Booking + 28-32w + DeliverySyphilis, HBV, HCV, HIV VL, GC/CT (If Risk), HSV (If Lesions)
PrEP UsersEvery 3 MonthsHIV Ag/Ab, Syphilis, GC/CT (3-Site if MSM), HCV (If Risk), Renal Function
Key: 3-Site Testing (Urethral, Pharyngeal, Rectal) for GC/CT in MSM; Syphilis Every 3-6m in MSM; HCV RNA Annually if Risk

3️⃣ STI Co-infection Management in HIV

Syphilis in HIV

AspectHIV-Specific Management
SerologyAtypical: Lower RPR Titres, Delayed Seroconversion, Prozone Phenomenon, Biologic False Positives (BFP) More Common, IgM Less Reliable
ClinicalMore Severe: Multiple Chancres, Rapid Progression, Neurosyphilis More Common/At Earlier Stage, Ocular Syphilis, Relapse After Treatment
CSF ExaminationMandatory at ALL Stages (Higher Neurosyphilis Risk, Atypical CSF)
TreatmentSame Penicillin Regimens; Longer Follow-Up (RPR 3, 6, 12, 24 Months); CSF at 6 Months if Neurosyphilis
Jarisch-HerxheimerMore Frequent/Severe (Cytokine Dysregulation) — Prednisolone Prophylaxis Considered
Treatment FailureMore Common — CSF Mandatory if RPR No 4-Fold Decline by 12 Months

HSV in HIV

AspectHIV-Specific Management
ClinicalMore Severe/Extensive/Chronic: Deep Ulcers, Mucosal Involvement, Perianal/Rectal, Disseminated, Oesophagitis, Encephalitis
RecurrencesMore Frequent, Prolonged, Severe
ResistanceTK Mutations More Common → Acyclovir/Valacyclovir/Famciclovir Cross-Resistance
TreatmentEpisodic: Acyclovir 400mg 5x Daily × 7-10d (Higher Dose/Longer); Suppressive: Valacyclovir 500mg BD (Preferred); Resistant → Foscarnet IV 40mg/kg q8h
Suppressive TherapyIndicated for All HIV+ with HSV-2 (Reduces Recurrences, Shedding, HIV Transmission); Valacyclovir 500mg BD

HPV in HIV

AspectHIV-Specific Management
Cervical/Anal DiseaseHigher Prevalence, Multiple Types, ↑ Persistence, Faster CIN2+ Progression
ScreeningCervical: Annual Cytology + HPV (vs 3-5y General); Anal: Annual Cytology + HPV (MSM/HIV+)
Vaccination9vHPV: 3 Doses (0, 2, 6m) Regardless of Age; Catch-Up to Age 26 (MSM to 26)
TreatmentCIN: Same Excision (LLETZ); Warts: More Extensive/Refractory → Surgical Preferred; Recurrence Higher

HCV in HIV

AspectHIV-Specific Management
DAA TherapySame Regimens/Durations as Mono-Infected (SOF/VEL 12w, GLE/PIB 8w/12w, SOF/VEL/VOX 12w Retreatment)
SVR Rates>95% (Same as Mono-Infected with Modern DAAs)
Drug InteractionsCritical: Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir ↔ Rifampicin (Contraindicated); Sofosbuvir/Velpatasvir ↔ Certain ART (Check Liverpool Database)
ART Regimen ChoiceIntegrase Inhibitor Based (DTG, BIC, RAL) Preferred (Fewest DAA Interactions)
ReinfectionHigher Risk (Continued Risk Behaviours) — Annual HCV RNA Screen

Mpox in HIV

AspectHIV-Specific Management
SeverityCD4<200 = Severe, Prolonged, Disseminated, Higher Mortality
AntiviralTecovirimat Indicated (CD4<200/Severe); Drug Interactions (Tecovirimat ↔ CYP3A4 Inducers/Inhibitors — Rifampicin, EFV, Boosted PIs)
VaccinationMVA-BN Safe (2 Doses) — Prioritise HIV+ (CD4<200)
ARTContinue/Initiate ART (Immune Reconstitution Critical)

3️⃣ ART & STI Drug Interactions — Critical Reference

Key Interaction Principles

MechanismART Classes AffectedSTI Drugs AffectedClinical Significance
CYP3A4 InhibitionBoosted PIs (DRV/r, ATV/r, LPV/r), CobicistatTecovirimat, DAA (GLE/PIB, SOF/VEL), Azole Antifungals, Azithromycin↑ STI Drug Levels → Toxicity or ↑ ART Levels → Toxicity
CYP3A4 InductionEFV, NVP, ETV, RPV, Rifampicin (TB Tx)Tecovirimat, DAA (GLE/PIB, SOF/VEL), Azole Antifungals↓ STI Drug Levels → Treatment Failure
Renal Tubular SecretionTDF, TAF, 3TC, FTCAcyclovir, Valacyclovir, Ganciclovir, Valganciclovir, Tenofovir↑ Renal Toxicity (Monitor eGFR)
Bone Marrow SuppressionZDV, TMP-SMXGanciclovir, Valganciclovir↑ Neutropenia/Anemia

High-Risk Interaction Pairs

ARTSTI DrugInteractionManagement
Boosted PIs (DRV/r, ATV/r)Tecovirimat↑ Tecovirimat (CYP3A4 Inhibition)Monitor Tecovirimat Toxicity; Consider Dose Reduction
EFV/NVPTecovirimat↓ Tecovirimat (CYP3A4 Induction)Avoid Combination; Use Alternative Antiviral
Boosted PIsGlecaprevir/Pibrentasvir↑ DAA LevelsAvoid; Use SOF/VEL Instead
DTG/BIC/RALMost STI DrugsMinimal InteractionsPreferred ART for STI Co-treatment
Rifampicin (TB Tx)All DAAs, Tecovirimat, Azole AntifungalsStrong Induction → Treatment FailureAvoid; Use Rifabutin or Alternative TB Regimen
Key: DTG/BIC/RAL-Based ART = Fewest STI Drug Interactions; Always Check Liverpool HIV Drug Interactions Database

4️⃣ Immune Reconstitution Inflammatory Syndrome (IRIS) — STI-Related

IRIS Definition

Paradoxical Worsening or New Onset of Symptoms after ART Initiation due to Immune Reconstitution unmasking subclinical infection or exaggerated inflammatory response.

STI-Associated IRIS

STIIRIS PresentationTimingManagement
M. tuberculosis (TB-IRIS)Fevers, Lymphadenopathy, Worsening CXR, New Lesions2-8 Weeks Post-ARTContinue ART + TB Tx; Steroids (Prednisolone 1.5mg/kg) if Severe
Herpes Simplex (HSV-IRIS)Severe Mucocutaneous Ulcers, Oesophagitis, Encephalitis2-12 WeeksContinue ART + Antivirals (Higher Dose/Longer); Steroids if Severe
Varicella Zoster (VZV-IRIS)Disseminated Shingles, Meningitis4-12 WeeksIV Acyclovir/Foscarnet + Steroids
Cryptococcal Meningitis (CM-IRIS)Worsening Headache, ICP ↑, Neurological Decline2-10 WeeksART Delay if Possible; Steroids + Amphotericin/Flucytosine
Syphilis (Syphilis-IRIS)Worsening RPR, New Lesions, Neurosyphilis Flare4-12 WeeksContinue ART + Penicillin; Steroids if Neurosyphilis Flare
HPV (Warts/CIN-IRIS)Rapid Wart Growth, New CIN Lesions3-12 MonthsStandard Treatment (Excision/Imiquimod); Continue ART
HCV (Hepatitis Flare)ALT Flare >5x ULN Post-ART4-12 WeeksMonitor; Continue ART + DAA if Indicated; Steroids Rarely Needed

IRIS Diagnostic Criteria (General)

  1. New/Worsening Symptoms after ART Initiation
  2. Temporal Association (Typically 2-12 Weeks Post-ART)
  3. CD4 Rise / HIV VL Drop (Immune Reconstitution)
  4. Exclusion of Drug Toxicity, Treatment Failure, New Infection

IRIS Management Principles

  1. Continue ART (Except Life-Threatening CM-IRIS → Delay ART if Possible)
  2. Treat Underlying Infection (Appropriate Antimicrobials)
  3. Anti-Inflammatory (Corticosteroids if Severe/Organ-Threatening)
  4. Monitor (Clinical, Lab, Imaging)

4️⃣ PrEP/PEP Impact on STI Epidemiology

PrEP (Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis) for HIV

RegimenEfficacySTI Impact
TDF/FTC Daily>99% (Adherent)↑ STI Incidence (Risk Compensation: ↓ Condom Use, ↑ Partners)
Event-Driven (2-1-1)High (MSM)Similar Risk Compensation
Cabotegravir LA (Bimonthly IM)>99%STI Monitoring Critical (No Daily Pill Reminder)

PrEP & STI Monitoring

RecommendationFrequency
HIV TestingEvery 3 Months
Syphilis (RPR/VDRL+TPPA)Every 3 Months
GC/CT (3-Site if MSM)Every 3 Months
HCV RNAAnnually (If Risk)
Renal Function (eGFR)Every 6 Months (TDF/FTC)
STI CounsellingEvery Visit (Condoms, Vaccination, Doxy-PEP)

Doxy-PEP (Post-Exposure Doxycycline for Bacterial STIs)

RegimenEfficacyPopulation
Doxycycline 200mg Single Dose Within 72h Post-SexGC ↓55%, CT ↓88%, Syphilis ↓87% (DoxyPEP Trial)MSM, Trans Women on PrEP or Living with HIV
CaveatsTetracycline Resistance (M. genitalium, Commensals), GI Side Effects, PhotosensitivityNot Standard of Care Yet (Guidelines Emerging)

4️⃣ STI Management in Specific HIV Clinical Scenarios

Acute HIV Infection (Primary HIV)

STI ScreeningTiming
Full STI ScreenAt HIV Diagnosis (Syphilis, HCV, HBV, HSV, GC/CT 3-Site, Trichomonas, HPV)
HCV RNAAt Diagnosis + 6 Months (Acute HCV Co-infection Possible)
Syphilis RPR/VDRLAt Diagnosis + 3 Months (Window Period)

Advanced HIV (CD4<200)

STIManagement Priority
OIs (PCP, Toxo, Crypto, MAC)Priority 1 (Life-Threatening)
SyphilisCSF Mandatory, Penicillin, Monitor RPR Monthly × 3
HSVSuppressive Valacyclovir 500mg BD (Reduce Reactivation)
CMVGanciclovir/Valganciclovir (Retinitis/Colitis)
MpoxTecovirimat Indicated
Cryptosporidium/MicrosporidiaART = Definitive Therapy; Nitazoxanide/Albendazole Adjunct

5️⃣ STI & ART Drug-Drug Interaction Quick Reference

STI DrugKey ART InteractionsMonitoring
Tecovirimat↑ with CYP3A4 Inhibitors (Boosted PIs, Cobicistat); ↓ with CYP3A4 Inducers (EFV, Rifampicin)LFT, Tecovirimat Level if Available
Glecaprevir/PibrentasvirContraindicated with Rifampicin; ↑ with Boosted PIsLFT, HCV RNA
Sofosbuvir/Velpatasvir↓ with EFV/Rifampicin; Few Interactions with INSTIHCV RNA, Renal Function (SOF)
Acyclovir/Valacyclovir↑ Renal Toxicity with TDF/TAFRenal Function (eGFR)
Ganciclovir/Valganciclovir↑ Myelosuppression with ZDV/TMP-SMXCBC, Renal Function
Foscarnet↑ Renal Toxicity with TDF/TAF, AminoglycosidesRenal Function, Electrolytes (Ca, Mg, K, P)
AzithromycinQT Prolongation with Certain ART (RPV, LPV/r)ECG if Risk Factors
Fluconazole↑ Levels with Boosted PIs; CYP3A4 InhibitionLFT, Drug Levels if Available
Rule of Thumb: INSTI-Based ART (DTG, BIC, RAL) = Fewest STI Drug Interactions; Always Check Liverpool HIV Drug Interactions Database Before Co-Prescribing

3. ⚡ FCPS/MRCP High-Yield Summary

TopicKey Points
HIV-STI Synergy↑ HIV Acquisition via STI Ulcers/Inflammation; ↑ STI Severity/Reactivation in HIV; Shared Risk Networks (MSM, Chemsex)
Syphilis in HIVAtypical Serology (Low RPR, Prozone, BFP); Neurosyphilis More Common/Earlier; CSF Mandatory All Stages; Same Penicillin Tx, Longer F/U
HSV in HIVSevere/Extensive/Recurrent; TK Resistance More Common; Suppressive Valacyclovir 500mg BD Indicated for All HIV+
HPV in HIV↑ Persistence, ↑ CIN2+, ↑ Anal Cancer (MSM); Annual Anal Cytology+HPV (MSM/HIV+); 3-Dose 9vHPV Regardless Age
HCV in HIVDAA Same Regimens (SOF/VEL 12w, GLE/PIB 8w); Check ART Interactions (Liverpool DB); Reinfection Risk Higher
Mpox in HIVCD4<200 = Severe; Tecovirimat Indicated; MVA-BN Safe; ART Continue
IRISParadoxical Worsening Post-ART; TB-IRIS, HSV-IRIS, Syphilis-IRIS, CM-IRIS; Continue ART + Antimicrobials + Steroids if Severe
Drug InteractionsTecovirimat ↔ CYP3A4 (Boosted PIs ↑, EFV ↓); DAA ↔ Rifampicin (Contraindicated); DTG/BIC/RAL = Fewest Interactions
PrEP & STIsRisk Compensation → ↑ STIs; 3-Monthly Screen (HIV, Syphilis, GC/CT 3-Site, HCV); Doxy-PEP Emerging (MSM/Trans Women on PrEP/HIV)
Screening in HIVMSM: 3-Site GC/CT q3-6m, Syphilis q3-6m, HCV RNA Annually; Pregnant: Booking + 28-32w + Delivery

4. 🎤 Viva Questions (Expected Answers)

#QuestionExpected Answer
1Syphilis in HIV — serological differences?Atypical: Lower RPR Titres, Delayed Seroconversion, Prozone (False -ve at High Titre), More BFP, IgM Less Reliable
2Neurosyphilis in HIV — CSF indication?Mandatory at ALL Stages (Higher Risk, Atypical Presentation, Earlier Onset)
3HSV in HIV — suppressive therapy indication?All HIV+ with HSV-2 (Reduces Recurrences, Shedding, HIV Transmission); Valacyclovir 500mg BD
4. HCV in HIV — DAA regimen choice?Same as Mono-Infected; GLE/PIB 8w (Non-Cirrhotics, Renal Safe), SOF/VEL 12w (All, Decompensated w/ RBV); Check ART Interactions (Liverpool DB)
5. Mpox in HIV — when to treat with tecovirimat?CD4<200 or Severe Disease → Tecovirimat 600mg BD × 14d; Check ART Interactions (CYP3A4)
6. IRIS — definition and common STI-associated types?Paradoxical Worsening/Unmasking Post-ART; TB-IRIS, HSV-IRIS, Syphilis-IRIS, CM-IRIS, HCV Flare
7. IRIS management — key principles?Continue ART (Except Life-Threatening CM-IRIS); Treat Underlying Infection; Steroids if Severe/Organ-Threatening
8. PrEP & STI monitoring — what and how often?HIV q3m, Syphilis q3m, GC/CT 3-Site q3m, HCV RNA Annually, Renal q6m
9. Doxy-PEP — efficacy and population?GC ↓55%, CT ↓88%, Syphilis ↓87% (DoxyPEP Trial); MSM, Trans Women on PrEP or HIV+
10. ART choice for STI co-treatment — preferred?INSTI-Based (DTG, BIC, RAL) = Fewest STI Drug Interactions; Always Check Liverpool HIV Drug Interactions Database

5. 🧩 Confusions & Mnemonics

ConfusionClarification
"HIV = Always Worse STI Outcomes"Mostly True, but ART Restores Immunity → IRIS = Paradoxical Worsening (Not Treatment Failure)
"HIV = Worse Syphilis Serology"Yes (Atypical) but Penicillin Still Works; CSF Mandatory All Stages
"HIV = Worse HSV, No Suppressive Therapy"NO. Suppressive Valacyclovir 500mg BD Indicated for ALL HIV+ with HSV-2
"HCV DAA = Different in HIV"NO. Same Regimens/Durations; Just Check ART Interactions (Liverpool DB)
"IRIS = Treatment Failure"NO. Paradoxical Immune Reconstitution → Continue ART + Treat Infection + Steroids if Severe
"IRIS = Stop ART"NO. Continue ART (Except Life-Threatening CM-IRIS Where Delay May Be Needed)
"PrEP = No STI Risk"NO. Risk Compensation → ↑ STIs (GC/CT/Syphilis ↑ in PrEP Users)
"Doxy-PEP = Standard of Care"NO. Emerging Evidence (DoxyPEP Trial); Guidelines Emerging; Resistance Concerns (M. genitalium)
"All ART = Same STI Interactions"NO. INSTI (DTG/BIC/RAL) = Fewest Interactions; Boosted PIs = Most Interactions
"Mpox = No Vaccine for HIV+"NO. MVA-BN Safe (2 Doses) for HIV+ (Even CD4<200); ACAM2000 Contraindicated
Mnemonic: HIV-STI SYNERGY MASTER
HIV-STI Synergy: Biological (Mucosal Disruption, Immunosuppression) + Epidemiological (Shared Networks)
In HIV: STIs More Severe, Atypical, Faster Progression, Higher Reactivation
Viral STIs in HIV: HSV (Severe, Suppressive Valacyclovir 500mg BD), HPV (Annual Anal Cyto+HPV), HCV (DAA Same Regimens, Check Interactions)
Syphilis in HIV: Atypical Serology (Low RPR, Prozone, BFP), Neurosyphilis Common → LP Mandatory All Stages
IRIS: Paradoxical Worsening Post-ART (TB, HSV, Syphilis, Crypto, CMV, HCV Flare) → Continue ART + Antimicrobials + Steroids if Severe
Drug Interactions: Tecovirimat ↔ CYP3A4 (PIs ↑, EFV ↓); DAAs ↔ Rifampicin (Contra); INSTI = Fewest Interactions
PrEP & STIs: Risk Compensation → ↑ STIs; 3m Screen (HIV, Syphilis, GC/CT 3-Site, HCV); Doxy-PEP Emerging
Screening in HIV: MSM: 3-Site GC/CT q3-6m, Syphilis q3-6m, HCV RNA Annually
Y (Why Integrated Services?): Concentric Epidemics, Shared Risk, Efficiency, Better Outcomes
Neurosyphilis HIV: LP Mandatory All Stages; Penicillin G IV 18-24MU/d × 10-14d
EPI/PEP: Doxy-PEP (200mg ≤72h Post-Sex) → GC↓55%, CT↓88%, Syphilis↓87% (MSM/Trans Women on PrEP/HIV)
Refrence: Liverpool HIV Drug Interactions Database = Gold Standard for ART-STI Co-Prescribing Check
Guidelines: WHO/BHIVA/EACS/CDC → Integrated STI/HIV Services, Combined Screening, Shared Decision-Making

9. 💬 Exam Answer Modes

FormatPromptKey Points
Long Essay"Describe the interaction between HIV and other STIs, including screening, management, and drug interactions."HIV-STI Synergy (Biological/Epidemiological); Syphilis in HIV (Atypical Serology, Neurosyphilis, LP Mandatory); HSV in HIV (Suppressive Valacyclovir Indicated); HPV/HCV/Mpox in HIV; IRIS (TB, HSV, Syphilis, Crypto) — Continue ART + Treat + Steroids; Drug Interactions (Tecovirimat/DAAs ↔ ART, Liverpool DB); PrEP & STI Risk Compensation; Integrated Screening (3-Site MSM, Annual HCV, Syphilis q3-6m)
Short Note"Syphilis in HIV — serological atypicalities and management."Atypical Serology: Low RPR, Delayed Seroconversion, Prozone, BFP; Neurosyphilis More Common → LP Mandatory All Stages; Treatment: Same Penicillin Regimens; Follow-Up: RPR Monthly × 3, Then 3-Monthly; CSF at 6m if Neurosyphilis
Viva"HIV+ MSM on TDF/FTC/DTG PrEP presents with Mpox. CD4 350. Management?"Mild Mpox (CD4>200) → Supportive Care + Isolation; No Tecovirimat Indicated; MVA-BN Vaccination (2 Doses 28d Apart) if Not Vaccinated; Continue PrEP (DTG Few Interactions); Contact Tracing + PEP for High-Risk Contacts; STI Screen (Syphilis, GC/CT 3-Site, HCV)
Ward Round"HIV+ patient (CD4 80) on ART 4 weeks develops new genital ulcers, fever, worsening RPR. Diagnosis & Management?"Syphilis IRIS (Paradoxical Worsening Post-ART) → Continue ART + Continue Penicillin (Stage-Appropriate); Supportive Care; Consider Steroids if Severe Neurosyphilis Flare; Monitor RPR Monthly; CSF if Neurological Signs
Last-Night"HIV-STI: Synergy (Mucosal/Immuno). Syphilis: Atypical Serology (Low RPR/Prozone/BFP), Neuro Common, LP All Stages, BPC Same, F/U Longer. HSV: Severe, Suppressive Valacyclovir 500mg BD All HIV+. HPV: Annual Anal Cyto/HPV, 3-Dose 9vHPV Any Age. HCV: DAA Same (SOF/VEL 12w, GLE/PIB 8w), Check Interactions. Mpox CD4<200 Severe, Tecovirimat. IRIS: Paradoxical Post-ART (TB/HSV/Syphilis/Crypto/CMV/HCV) → Continue ART + Treat + Steroids if Severe. Interactions: Tecovirimat↔CYP3A4 (PIs↑, EFV↓), DAAs↔Rifampicin (Contra), DTG/BIC/RAL=Fewest. PrEP: 3m Screen HIV/Syph/GC/CT/HCV, Risk Compens↑STIs, Doxy-PEP Emerging. Screen MSM: 3-Site GC/CT q3-6m, Syph q3-6m, HCV RNA Annual."Compressed.

10. 📌 Summary

  • HIV-STI Synergy: Biological (Mucosal Disruption, Immunosuppression) + Epidemiological (Shared Risk Networks) → ↑ Acquisition, Severity, Reactivation
  • Syphilis in HIV: Atypical Serology (Low RPR, Prozone, BFP), Neurosyphilis More Common → LP Mandatory All Stages; Same Penicillin Regimens, Longer Follow-Up (RPR Monthly × 3, CSF at 6m)
  • HSV in HIV: Severe, Extensive, Recurrent, TK Resistance → Suppressive Valacyclovir 500mg BD Indicated for ALL HIV+
  • HPV in HIV: ↑ Persistence, CIN2+, Anal Cancer → Annual Anal Cytology+HPV (MSM/HIV+), 3-Dose 9vHPV Any Age
  • HCV in HIV: DAA Same Regimens (SOF/VEL 12w, GLE/PIB 8w/12w, SOF/VEL/VOX 12w) — Check ART Interactions (Liverpool Database)
  • Mpox in HIV: CD4<200 = Severe → Tecovirimat Indicated; MVA-BN Safe (2 Doses); ART Continue
  • IRIS: Paradoxical Worsening Post-ART (TB, HSV, Syphilis, CM, Crypto, HCV Flare) → Continue ART + Treat Infection + Steroids if Severe/Organ-Threatening
  • Drug Interactions: Tecovirimat ↔ CYP3A4 (Boosted PIs ↑, EFV ↓); DAAs ↔ Rifampicin (Contraindicated); INSTI (DTG/BIC/RAL) = Fewest Interactions — Always Check Liverpool HIV Drug Interactions Database
  • PrEP & STIs: Risk Compensation → ↑ STI Incidence; 3-Monthly Screening (HIV, Syphilis, GC/CT 3-Site, HCV RNA); Doxy-PEP Emerging (GC↓55%, CT↓88%, Syphilis↓87%)
  • Screening in HIV: MSM: 3-Site GC/CT q3-6m, Syphilis q3-6m, HCV RNA Annually; Pregnant: Booking + 28-32w + Delivery; All HIV+: Annual Full STI Screen

11. ❓ MCQs (10)

  1. Syphilis in HIV — serological hallmark?

A. High RPR Titres B. Atypical: Low RPR, Prozone, Biologic False Positives C. Rapid Seroconversion D. Negative TPPA

Answer: B. Atypical Serology: Low/Erratic RPR, Prozone Effect, More BFP, Delayed Seroconversion.

  1. Neurosyphilis in HIV — CSF examination indication?

A. Only if Neurological Symptoms B. Mandatory at ALL Stages C. Only Late Latent D. Only if RPR >1:32

Answer: B. LP Mandatory at ALL Stages in HIV (Higher Neurosyphilis Risk, Atypical Presentation).

  1. HSV suppressive therapy in HIV — indication?

A. Only if ≥10 Recurrences/Year B. All HIV+ with HSV-2 C. Only if CD4<200 D. Not Recommended

Answer: B. Suppressive Valacyclovir 500mg BD Indicated for ALL HIV+ with HSV-2 (Reduces Shedding, Recurrence, HIV Transmission).

  1. HCV DAA regimen in HIV — same as mono-infected?

A. No, Different B. Yes, Same Regimens/Durations (Check ART Interactions) C. Only IFN-Based D. Only Ribavirin-Based

Answer: B. Same Pan-Genotypic Regimens (SOF/VEL 12w, GLE/PIB 8w/12w, SOF/VEL/VOX 12w); Check ART Interactions (Liverpool DB).

  1. Mpox in HIV — tecovirimat indication?

A. All HIV+ B. CD4<200 or Severe Disease C. Only CD4<50 D. Never Answer: B. CD4<200 or Severe Disease → Tecovirimat 600mg BD × 14d (Check CYP3A4 Interactions).

  1. IRIS definition?

A. Treatment Failure B. Paradoxical Worsening/Unmasking Post-ART Due to Immune Reconstitution C. Drug Toxicity D. New Infection Answer: B. Paradoxical Worsening/Unmasking Within Weeks of ART Initiation Due to Immune Reconstitution.

  1. IRIS management — key principle?

A. Stop ART B. Continue ART + Treat Underlying Infection + Steroids if Severe/Organ-Threatening C. Stop ART + Treat D. Reduce ART Dose

Answer: B. Continue ART (Except Life-Threatening CM-IRIS) + Treat Infection + Steroids if Severe.

  1. PrEP & STI monitoring — recommended frequency for MSM?

A. Annually B. Every 3 Months (HIV, Syphilis, GC/CT 3-Site, HCV RNA Annually, Renal q6m) C. Every 6 Months D. Only at Initiation

Answer: B. Every 3 Months (HIV Ag/Ab, Syphilis RPR/VDRL+TPPA, GC/CT 3-Site NAAT, HCV RNA Annually, Renal q6m).

  1. Tecovirimat ART interaction — boosted PIs?

A. ↓ Tecovirimat B. ↑ Tecovirimat (CYP3A4 Inhibition) C. No Interaction D. Contraindicated

Answer: B. Boosted PIs (CYP3A4 Inhibitors) → ↑ Tecovirimat Levels; Monitor Toxicity/Dose Adjust.

  1. Doxy-PEP — efficacy for bacterial STIs?

A. GC ↓90%, CT ↓90%, Syphilis ↓90% B. GC ↓55%, CT ↓88%, Syphilis ↓87% (DoxyPEP Trial) C. No Significant Reduction D. Only Syphilis Reduced

Answer: B. DoxyPEP Trial: GC ↓55%, CT ↓88%, Syphilis ↓87% (MSM/Trans Women on PrEP/HIV).

12. 📋 SBAs (10)

  1. HIV+ MSM, CD4 350, on TDF/FTC/DTG PrEP, diagnosed with Mpox. Management?

A. Tecovirimat 600mg BD × 14d B. Supportive Care + Isolation (Mild, CD4>200) C. MVA-BN Vaccine Only D. Ceftriaxone

Answer: B. Mild Mpox (CD4>200) → Supportive Care + Isolation; Tecovirimat Only if Severe/High-Risk.

  1. HIV+ patient on ART 4 weeks develops fever, worsening RPR, new rash. Diagnosis?

A. Drug Rash B. Syphilis IRIS C. Acute HIV Seroconversion D. Secondary Syphilis

Answer: B. Syphilis IRIS (Paradoxical Worsening 2-12w Post-ART); Continue ART + Penicillin + Consider Steroids.

  1. HIV+ patient with HSV-2, 8 recurrences/year. Suppressive therapy?

A. Acyclovir 400mg BD B. Valacyclovir 500mg OD (or BD) C. No Suppression D. Foscarnet

Answer: B. Valacyclovir 500mg OD (Preferred) or Acyclovir 400mg BD; Indicated for ALL HIV+ with HSV-2.

  1. HIV+ patient on efavirenz-based ART needs HCV DAA. Preferred regimen?

A. Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir B. Sofosbuvir/Velpatasvir (Fewer Interactions with EFV) C. SOF/VEL/VOX D. Interferon+Ribavirin

Answer: B. SOF/VEL Preferred with EFV (GLE/PIB Contraindicated with Rifampicin, EFV Significant Interaction).

  1. HIV+ MSM on PrEP, 3-month STI screen. Tests?

A. HIV Only B. HIV + Syphilis C. HIV + Syphilis + 3-Site GC/CT NAAT + HCV RNA (Annually) D. HIV + HCV Only

Answer: C. 3-Site GC/CT + Syphilis + HCV RNA (Annually) + Renal Function q6m.

13. 🔑 Answer Keys

MCQsSBAs
1-B, 2-B, 3-B, 4-B, 5-B, 6-B, 7-B, 8-B, 9-B, 10-B1-B, 2-B, 3-B, 4-B, 5-B
